arts.ttl: McTominay goal gives Serie A leader Napoli win over Torino as Lazio loses ground
arts.sum: Napoli ground out a 1-0 win at Torino to ensure it will end the weekend still top of a tight Serie A table, while at least one of its opponents dropped points on Sunday. Scott McTominay scored the only goal of the match in the first half as Napoli pulled away from one of its closest rivals as Lazio lost 3-1 at relegation-threatened Parma.

arts.ttl: Pecchia: ‘Lazio are a team of possession and vertical play’
arts.sum: In a pre-match press conference ahead of Parma’s upcoming clash with Lazio, manager Fabio Pecchia shared his thoughts on team strategy and the challenges they face. The focus of the discussion centered around the team’s defensive vulnerabilities, particularly concerning goals conceded from high balls. Pecchia addressed concerns about the team’s defensive performance, stating, “For Retegui’s goal, we started from a lost ball and had lost our markings. We need to pay more attention. We’re conceding a few more crosses, but we’re in line with the Serie A average.” When questioned about potentially adopting a more conservative approach against Lazio, known The post Pecchia: ‘Lazio are a team of possession and vertical play’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Parma v Lazio: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Lazio prepare for their upcoming match with several notable changes to their starting lineup. Nuno Tavares, the Serie A assist leader, remains sidelined, with Luca Pellegrini expected to continue at left-back. Several crucial players are set to make their comeback to the starting eleven. Nicolo Rovella, who was rested in the Europa League, will return to midfield. Top scorer Taty Castellanos, first-choice goalkeeper Ivan Provedel, and captain Mattia Zaccagni are also expected to reclaim their positions. Zaccagni enters the match with remarkable momentum, having scored in his last three league games against Cagliari, Monza, and Bologna. arts.ttl: Lazio will look for continuity against Parma
arts.sum: Lazio have won each of their last nine Serie A games against Parma between 2014 and 2021; only against Udinese have they gone on a longer such streak (10 wins in a row between 1998 and 2003) in their top-flight history. Parma have lost each of their last nine Serie A games against Lazio, with an aggregate score of 21-4 for the Biancocelesti; it’s their longest streak of successive defeats ever suffered against a single opponent in the top-flight.
